title: Images, Layers, Manifests, Tags, Digests, and Registry Identity · Container-System Defense
summary: A release board compares fresh images, layers, manifests, tags, digests, and registry identity decisions and accepts only answers tied to observable image, container, build, or Compose evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for image identity and layer provenance ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.
Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers
steps.instruction:
Order the Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers container delivery evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers, keep Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A quiz release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior. Competency probe: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Every Dockerfile instruction produces one filesystem layer containing the whole image.
